channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The best iPad deals you can get right now
item.description: While the best iPad deals usually land during major sale events like Black Friday, many great iPad deals are available outside of those moments. The day-to-day discounts come and go like the changing winds, so there’s often some amount to be saved somewhere, particularly on Apple’s most affordable iPad and the latest iPad Mini. Hell, you […]

item.title: 'Building AI literacy, proficiency, and fluency': how the US National Science Foundation is planning a nationwide AI workforce expansion
item.description: NSF outlines its nationwide plan to expand AI literacy, training, and adoption for workers, businesses, and local communities.

item.title: Be careful what you click - hackers use Claude Code leak to push malware
item.description: Fake source code repositories carrying infostealers are popping up on GitHub.

item.title: Meta Smart Glasses Can Now Track All the Food You Put Into Your Mouth
item.description: Whether people want to give Meta all their food data is another question entirely.

item.title: Much like the game’s zombies, State of Decay 3 is somehow still alive
item.description: Amid Microsoft's hacking and slashing of its Xbox division, you wouldn't be crazy for thinking State of Decay 3 was dead. After all, the title was announced nearly six years ago, and, well, we haven't heard much since. But the survival game is still in the pipeline, and developer Undead Labs will hold a series of playtests beginning in May.In the announcement, franchise co-creator Brant Fitzgerald emphasized the role of community input in the game's continued development. "We've read your feedback on Discord, we've watched your gameplay clips and livestreams on YouTube," he said. "It became cl...

item.title: Here’s Who the Mysterious Main Characters Are in ‘Disclosure Day’
item.description: The new Steven Spielberg sci-fi action movie stars Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor, Colin Firth, and Colman Domingo.

item.title: 3 unmissable new Prime Video shows to stream in April 2026 — including the final season of The Boys
item.description: The Boys season 5 leads April's Prime Video shows list, but there's two other shows you shouldn't miss either.

item.title: I went back 50 years and played the best Apple-1 games — and my brain is still recovering
item.description: With Apple turning 50 this week, I decided there was only one way to celebrate — melt my brain by playing some of the finest games made for the Apple-1. Here's how I got on.

item.title: Rubin Observatory Spots Over 11,000 Asteroids in Record Haul
item.description: Astronomers just can’t catch a break from Rubin.

item.title: The final days of the Tesla Model X and S are here. All bets are on the Cybercab.
item.description: It's a new era for Tesla — and one that will rely on its ability to launch the Cybercab and mass-produce the Optimus robot.

item.title: Volkswagen CEO Is Standing Up for Physical Buttons in Cars
item.description: CEO Thomas Schäfer calls physical controls in cars "non-negotiable" after years of infotainment dominance.

item.title: 32 Go de RAM et ray tracing boosté : la Nvidia GeForce RTX 6090 commence à faire parler d’elle
item.description: Nvidia n'a rien de prévu pour nos PC cette année, mais les labos de Santa Clara ne chôment pas. Une fuite massive détaille les ambitions de la future série RTX 60 : une bande passante doublée et un ray tracing enfin mature.

item.title: Lowe's just launched a massive spring sale — here's everything I'd buy for my backyard, including furniture, plants, grills, and more
item.description: The Lowe's 2026 SpringFest sale is live, and I'm rounding up all the best deals on backyard essentials, including patio furniture, grills, tools, lawnmowers, and more.

item.title: The $80,000 clue hiding in plain sight in U.S. healthcare
item.description: The diagnostic odyssey is costing more than we realize. American healthcare faces a persistent paradox: We have extraordinary medical technology, yet patients often spend years navigating a system that treats symptoms before identifying the underlying cause of disease.

item.title: Why employees are giving up remote work and moving back to urban centers
item.description: As stricter return‑to‑office mandates take hold, new data shows remote workers reversing their pandemic‑era migration. One of the major changes unleashed by the pandemic—and the accompanying spread of remote work—was the large migration of employees from major urban areas. With many jobs no longer anchored to city-based offices, people were free to move to almost anywhere else they preferred to live—often at lower costs to boot. But now, new survey data indicates that exodus has reversed course, with grim labor markets and tightening return-to-office (RTO) mandates causing employment-focused w...

item.title: Trump labor board tells Amazon to negotiate with Staten Island warehouse union
item.description: The Trump administration's labor board has ordered Amazon to recognize and bargain with the International Brotherhood of Teamsters union, which represents workers at a warehouse in Staten Island. This is just the latest chapter in a multiyear standoff between Staten Island warehouse workers and Amazon, according to a report by The Washington Post. The union has been trying to bring Amazon to the bargaining table for years to negotiate pay, benefits and workplace safety. The labor board's proclamation doesn't mean that the battle is over. It's highly likely this will be settled in court. An Ama...

item.title: That Teenage Bout of Mono May Have a Troubling Second Act
item.description: Scientists are uncovering a possible link between teenage mononucleosis and a chronic condition years later.

item.title: CorelDRAW Graphics Suite 2026 review: An impressive collection of creative software for PC and Mac — now with added AI
item.description: CorelDRAW Graphics Suite 2026 is here, and what does this powerful collection of software bring to the budding artist? Well a lot of AI actually. If you want to see if this new version is right for you, read on

item.title: 'Copilot is for entertainment purposes only': Even Microsoft's official terms and conditions say you really shouldn't be using its AI at work
item.description: Microsoft notes Copilot is for "entertainment purposes only" and to be used "at your own risk," but nothing has changed to its availability.

item.title: The Shark FlexStyle is our favorite Dyson Airwrap dupe, and its $160 off at Amazon right now
item.description: As of April 3, you can get the Shark FlexStyle Air Styling & Drying System (with a storage bag) for $219.99 at Amazon, down from $379.99.

item.title: We Landed on Mars, A.K.A. the Set of ‘For All Mankind’
item.description: io9 visited Mars Mission Control, located on a studio lot in Los Angeles, to talk season five of the Apple TV show.

item.title: Battlefield 6 audio director explains there's 'a lot of demand' for high-quality soundtracks — 'We're following suit with the rest of the entertainment industries'
item.description: Battlefield 6 audio director Jeff Wilson has discussed the game's award-winning sound in a new interview.

item.title: 'A guy who was not an expert...used ChatGPT to make a custom mRNA vaccine for his dog's cancer', and other incredible tales from Sam Altman
item.description: OpenAI CEO shared stories of some incredible ChatGPT and CODEX successes, but what do they mean for you (and your doing)?

item.title: Google dévoile 4 IA qui peuvent tourner sur votre smartphone ou votre ordinateur, sans Internet
item.description: Avec Gemma 4, Google propose 4 intelligences artificielles en open source, dotées chacune de performances spécifiques. L’entreprise américaine se positionne dorénavant comme un concurrent de poids face à des géants chinois comme DeepSeek et Qwen (Alibaba).

item.title: Europe’s cyber agency blames hacking gangs for massive data breach and leak
item.description: CERT-EU blamed the cybercrime group TeamPCP for the recent hack on the European Commission, and said the notorious ShinyHunters gang was responsible for leaking the stolen data online.

item.title: Apple releases iOS 26.5 beta without any sign of AI Siri
item.description: Developers can start testing the upcoming iOS 26.5 release.

item.title: What John Galliano going to Zara tells us about fashion—and everything else
item.description: It looks like fashion news. It’s actually a signal about the future of value creation itself. Fashion, it turns out, is a leading indicator. Long before mainstream business commentary catches up to a structural shift in the economy, the runway has usually already staged it. The announcement that John Galliano—arguably the greatest couturier alive—has signed a two-year creative partnership with Zara is one of those moments. It looks like fashion news. It is actually a signal about the future of value creation itself....

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is a black hole of entertainment
item.description: I realized something was genuinely wrong with The Super Mario Galaxy Movie about 30 minutes in: I hadn't laughed even once. My audience of around 15 people, including a few families, was dead silent as well. The guy sitting behind me, a Nintendo fan decked out in Mario gear, was so bored he fell asleep. Sure, this is made for kids, but as a Nintendo devotee myself, and someone who has to watch a ton of children's films on repeat, even the Despicable Me films are more entertaining. To be fair, there's the pretense of a plot: Koopa Jr. and Peach are on parallel tracks to reconnect with a sense o...

item.title: Amazon Prime Video en avril 2026 : c’est le grand final pour The Boys !
item.description: Amazon a dévoilé les nouveautés qui arrivent sur Prime Video en avril, et s'il y en a bien une qui est attendue avec impatience, c'est la saison finale de The Boys. Mais voyons également ce qu'il y a d'autre au programme.

item.title: Where is Artemis II now? This NASA tool lets you track the live flight of the Orion spacecraft on its moon journey
item.description: Think of it like a Google Maps for space. Unlike in 1972, you don’t have to be a high-tech agency to follow the latest lunar mission. For the first time since 1972, astronauts are on their way into deep space as part of NASA’s Artemis II mission.
